column1: # budgets.tsv - manifest for scripts/run_stress.sh (P4 extreme stress harness).
# TAB-separated. Lines starting with # are comments.
#   file      path relative to tests/stress/ (generated/ files are produced by
#             gen_stress_sources.sh; found/ files are XKNOWN minimal repros)
#   mode      r | aot | both      (execution paths to test)
#             run      - execute once per mode with budgets
#             jitcache - r-only: 50 sequential -r runs against one shared JIT
#                        cache dir; run 1 gets timeout_s (cold), runs 2..50 get
#             rep3     - run 3x per mode; all three stdouts must be identical
#                        AND pass the normal budget checks (flake detector)
#   timeout_s wall-clock ceiling per execution (AOT compile gets the same
#             ceiling separately from the binary run)
#   rss_r_mb  max resident set size ceiling for JIT -r runs (MB). JIT baseline
#             is ~222MB on macOS arm64 (stdlib object + LLVM), so r ceilings
#             sit above that floor by design.
#   rss_aot_mb max RSS ceiling for the AOT binary run (MB; baseline ~28MB)
#   xknown    - or the ESH ledger id of the documented-open bug this row pins
#             (failure -> XKNOWN, pass -> XPASS which FAILS the gate: stale
#             xknown entries must be promoted to normal rows)
#   expect    literal substring that must appear in stdout ("-" = no output

# rec_deep_nontco: non-tail native frames ~2KB each (real frame cost, not a leak). Sweep E (2026-07-03) re-calibrated the safe margin from 250k to 200k: the safe depth regressed (JIT 240k ok/250k SIGILL, AOT 200k ok/250k SIGILL) vs the 270k-first-fail calibration. 200k row is the passing margin; 250k row is the ESH-0112 crash pin.
# nested_parens_10k: JIT compile measured 35.8s / 6710MB + macro-depth ERROR spam (ESH-0103); AOT 0.73s / 93MB. r row XKNOWN until fixed.
# nested_parens_900: JIT compile of even 900-deep nesting measured 3.25s / 653MB (same ESH-0103 growth curve, below the spam limit); r ceiling pins it under 1GB.
# data_sort_50k: measured 983MB r / 985MB aot - stdlib sort allocates ~1GB for 50k elements (noted in ESH-0098); ceiling sized to it.
# num_div_zero: guard/exception path under -r measured 507MB (vs 218MB typical JIT baseline); ceiling sized to it.
